LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS STEVEN STAPP AND JUDE GOGAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING: ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION IN THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF NICOLE RIVERA IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO ALL FOUR CAUSES OF ACTION. NO OPPOSITION FILED. THE PREVAILING PARTY SHALL SUBMIT A [PROPOSED] WRITTEN ORDER THAT TRACKS THE COURT'S RULING, VERBATIM. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; REPORTER: NOT REPORTED

DISCOVERY 302, THE COURT CONFIRMS THE TENTATIVE RULING WITH MODIFICATIONS. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFF'S WRITTEN RESPONSES TO DOCUMENT REQUESTS, PLAINTIFF'S DOCUMENT PRODUCTION AND PLAINTIFF'S ATTENDANCE AT DEPOSITION AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. SFFCU'S REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF NICOLE RIVERA, IS GRANTED IN THE AMOUNT OF $1100. ALL OTHER REQUESTS FOR SANCTIONS, IF ANY, ARE DENIED. PRODUCTION COMPLIANCE DEADLINE IS SET AT 10 DAYS AFTER SERVICE OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF WRITTEN ORDER. DEPOSITION IS SET FOR DECEMBER 20, 2016 AT 9:30 AM AT DEFENSE COUNSEL'S OFFICE. WRITTEN ORDER IS SIGNED AND FILED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: DOUGLAS ROBBINS; CLERK: JOSE RIOS MERIDA; NOT REPORTED. (D302/JPT)

DISCOVERY 302, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING OR EVIDENTIARY SANCTIONS, TOGETHER WITH MONETARY SANCTIONS, BASED ON PLAINTIFF S VIOLATION OF COURT S DISCOVERY ORDER IS GRANTED IN PART. PLAINTIFF IS PRECLUDED FROM INTRODUCING INTO EVIDENCE ANY DOCUMENT RESPONSIVE TO ANY OF THE DOCUMENT REQUESTS CONTAINED IN DEFENDANT'S FIRST SET OF REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS TO PLAINTIFF, AND PLAINTIFF MAY NOT TESTIFY AT TRIAL OR SUBMIT A DECLARATION IN SUPPORT OF OR OPPOSITION TO A MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. PLAINTIFF SHALL PAY DEFENDANT $2,482 FOR THE REASONABLE ATTORNEYS' FEES AND COSTS INCURRED AS A RESULT OF PLAINTIFF'S VIOLATION OF THE COURT'S NOVEMBER 29, 2016 ORDER. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO-TEM: NAOMI JANE GRAY; CLERK: JOHNNY SENGMANY; NOT REPORTED.=302/JPT

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Aug-14-2017 Vacated

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jun-22-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Ian P. Fellerman, Esq. (510-337-2810) of Wiley Price & Radulovich, LLP for Defendant.
No other Appearance.

RULING - The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. No opposition filed. Order and Judgment signed. Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR 711, ckaren@sftc.org =(302/RBU)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Or Evidentiary Sanctions, Together With Monetary Sanctions, Based On Plaintiff S Violation Of Court S Discovery Order
Off Calendar Feb-01-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: ATTY IAN P. FELLERMAN OF WILEY PRICE & RADULOVICH, LLP TEL NO. 510-337-2810 FOR THE DEFEDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING- THE COURT ADOPTED IT'S TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S Motion For Terminating Or Evidentiary Sanctions, Together With Monetary Sanctions, Based On Plaintiff S Violation Of Court S Discovery Order IS GRANTED IN PART. Plaintiff is precluded from introducing into evidence any document responsive to any of the document requests contained in Defendant's first set of requests for production of documents to Plaintiff, and Plaintiff may not testify at trial or submit a declaration in support of or opposition to a motion for summary judgment or summary adjudication. Plaintiff shall pay Defendant $2,482 for the reasonable attorneys' fees and costs incurred as a result of Plaintiff's violation of the Court's November 29, 2016 Order. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge Pro-tem: Naomi Jane Gray; Clerk: JOHNNY SENGMANY; Not reported.=302/JPT

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-30-2016 Set For Trial On Sep-11-17 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Plaintiff S Written Responses To Document Requests, Plaintiff S Document Production And Plaintiff S Attendance At Deposition; And Request For Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Attorney, Ian P. Feldman of the law offices of Wiley Price & Radulovich LLP (510.337.2810) appears as counsel for defendant/moving party

No other appearance is made in this matter

The Court confirms the tentative ruling with modifications. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO FEDERAL CREDIT UNION'S Motion To Compel Plaintiff's Written Responses To Document Requests, Plaintiff's Document Production And Plaintiff's Attendance At Deposition And Request For Sanctions is GRANTED AS UNOPPOSED. SFFCU's request for monetary sanctions against Plaintiff Nicole Rivera, is GRANTED in the amount of $1100. All other requests for sanctions, if any, are DENIED. Production compliance deadline is set at 10 days after service of notice of entry of written order. Deposition is set for December 20, 2016 at 9:30 AM at defense counsel's office. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge Pro Tem: Douglas Robbins; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Not Reported. (D302/jpt)

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-06-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANTs STEVEN STAPP and JUDE GOGAN'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT, as to all four causes of action in the first amended complaint filed by plaintiff Nicole Rivera is SUSTAINED without leave to amend as to all four causes of action. No opposition filed. The prevailing party shall submit a [proposed] written order that tracks the Court's ruling, verbatim.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Jose Rios Merida; Reporter: Not Reported. (D302)
